name: pattern-detector
description: Detect design patterns and anti-patterns in code with recommendations.
capabilities: Identifies creational patterns like Singleton, Factory, Builder, and Prototype
Detects structural patterns including Adapter, Decorator, Facade, and Proxy
Recognizes behavioral patterns such as Observer, Strategy, Command, and State
Flags anti-patterns like God Object, Spaghetti Code, and Magic Numbers
Provides specific line numbers and refactoring recommendations
Evaluates pattern implementation quality and suggests improvements
author: CuriousLearner
securitySummary: This is a pure markdown prompt skill containing only documentation and code examples. No executable code, scripts, network calls, filesystem access, or command execution. The static analyzer triggered on legitimate software pattern names (Strategy, Command, State, Singleton, Builder) which are incorrectly flagged as cryptographic keywords. JavaScript template literals in code examples were misidentified as shell backticks. All 61 findings are false positives.
valueStatement: Poor code structure leads to technical debt and maintenance problems. This skill analyzes code to identify design patterns in use and anti-patterns that should be fixed, providing clear recommendations for improvement.
limitations: Analyzes only code shared in conversation, not entire codebases
Does not execute or modify any code
Cannot detect patterns across multiple files without context

useCases.title: Review Code Quality
useCases.description: Analyze your code to identify architectural issues and receive specific refactoring suggestions.

useCases.title: Assess Codebase Health
useCases.description: Evaluate team code for anti-patterns and ensure adherence to architectural standards.

useCases.title: Learn Pattern Recognition
useCases.description: Understand why certain patterns are problematic and how to fix them with practical examples.

promptTemplates.title: Analyze Code File
promptTemplates.prompt: Use @pattern-detector to analyze this code and identify all design patterns and anti-patterns present.

promptTemplates.title: Focus on Problems
promptTemplates.prompt: @pattern-detector --anti-patterns-only this code and explain each problem with severity level.

promptTemplates.title: Check Implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: Use @pattern-detector to check if this code follows proper pattern implementation practices.

promptTemplates.title: Get Recommendations
promptTemplates.prompt: @pattern-detector this code and provide specific recommendations for improving the architecture with code examples.

bestPractices: Start with anti-pattern detection to identify the most critical issues first
Focus refactoring efforts on God Objects and Spaghetti Code before optimizing patterns
Use pattern recommendations as a guide, not a mandate - context matters
antiPatterns: Using pattern-detector to justify over-engineering instead of keeping solutions simple
Ignoring severity levels and trying to fix low-priority issues first
Applying patterns without understanding the specific problem they solve

faq.question: What languages does this skill support?
faq.answer: Pattern detection works for any language with standard OOP patterns including JavaScript, Python, Java, and C++.

faq.question: How deep does the analysis go?
faq.answer: The skill analyzes code structure and organization. It does not execute code or perform static analysis.

faq.question: Can it integrate with my IDE?
faq.answer: This is an AI skill that works within Claude, Codex, and Claude Code conversations.

faq.question: Is my code data safe?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill only processes code you explicitly share in the conversation. No data is stored or transmitted.

faq.question: Why are some patterns marked questionable?
faq.answer: Patterns marked acceptable or questionable may be over-engineered or not ideal for the specific use case.

faq.question: How is this different from linters?
faq.answer: Linters check syntax and style. Pattern detection identifies architectural issues and design decisions at a higher level.
